message:
fix(codegen): Remove hardcoded .asString() conversion for PyValue arguments

Fixed a bug where PyValue arguments were hardcoded to use .asString()
conversion, breaking function calls expecting other types (int, float, etc.).

Changed from:
  __pv.asString()  // Always converted to string - WRONG!

To:
  Pass PyValue/unknown arguments directly without conversion

This fixes cases like Rat.__init__ calling gcd(den, num) where the
parameters are anytype and could be PyValue, but gcd expects i64.

The callee can handle the type appropriately via Zig's comptime type
system or anytype parameters.

Test impact: Removes immediate cause of Rat class failures, though
other issues may remain.
